text:
King's College Hospital 

Department of Neurosciences 

Clinic Date: 19 May 2018 

Dr Tomas 
Consultant Neurologist   
King's College Hospital  
Department of Neurosciences, King's College Hospital, London SE5 9RS  

Dear Dr Tomas 
Consultant Neurologist ,  

Sarah Mitchell, DOB: 14-03-1992, Hospital No: K842317 NHS No. 9076543312  
Flat 2B, 16 Wren Close, London, SE15 2LP

Thank you for asking me to review your patient, whom I saw in clinic today accompanied by her paid carer, who assists with day-to-day communication and with seizure and symptom logging. The consultation was conducted with the carer’s support and brief use of simplified questions. A communication passport was provided and cross-checked with the home seizure diary. Some characters in prior paperwork appeared garbled (e.g. Ã‚Â·, ï¿½, â€), but the clinical content was clear enough to corroborate the recent history.  

History: The carer reports that over the past two months there have been intermittent brief behavioural arrests noted mainly in the late afternoon, occasionally with loss of thread of conversation and a short pause in activity. There have been no consistent triggers identified; sleep has been variable, and hydration reminders are in place. She presented with dizziness and headaches on consecutive days, but these resolved spontaneously. On 15 Feb, following the use of her prescribed medication, the absences became less frequent. The last such episode occurred on 21 Feb and she has been stable since. Given her stability now, I do not propose further intervention for now.  

Examination: She was alert, engaged with prompting, and oriented to person and place with the carer’s cueing. Cranial nerves: normal eye movements, no nystagmus, pupils equal and reactive. Motor: normal tone and power throughout; no pronator drift. Coordination intact on finger–nose testing with demonstration first. Gait steady with mild dyspraxic features long-standing per carer. Vitals were within expected limits.  

Investigations: Review of prior routine EEG from last year was non-diagnostic; no new EEG performed today given current stability. Medication levels have not been recently checked; there have been no adverse effects reported beyond transient morning lethargy, now improved. No new imaging indicated at present.  

Current management and plan: 
- Continue current prescribed medication regimen as per primary care record; the carer will maintain the seizure and wellbeing diary using the same format for consistency. 
- Provide reasonable adjustments for communication at all appointments (simple language, written summaries, and allowing extra time). 
- Safety and support: ongoing adherence to the personalised care plan, including hydration reminders, sleep routine, and community learning disability nurse input as needed. 
- Red flags discussed with the carer: prolonged episodes, clusters, new injury, or any change in pattern; if these occur, please contact our service or escalate via urgent pathways. 

Follow-up: Given her stability since 21 Feb and the clear diary entries, I will review in 4–6 months, or sooner if there is any deterioration or if the diary indicates recurrence. At present I do not propose additional investigations.  

Please let me know if there is further information from school/day-centre staff or community teams that would assist, and we will integrate this into the record.  

Yours sincerely

The last such episode occurred on 21 Feb and she has been stable since. Given her stability now, I do not propose further intervention for now

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis:
- Identify current period: Clinic Date is 19 May 2018.
- Last recorded seizure: "The last such episode occurred on 21 Feb" and "she has been stable since," implying no seizures from 21 Feb to 19 May.
- Duration since last seizure: 21 Feb to 19 May ≈ 87 days ≈ 3 months.
- Per rule: Not seizure free ≥6 months, so do not label as seizure free; instead estimate frequency.
- There was 1 seizure within the last ~3 months → 1 per 3 month.
seizure_frequency_number: The last such episode occurred on 21 Feb and she has been stable since.
reference: She presented with dizziness and headaches on consecutive days, but these resolved spontaneously. Given her stability now, I do not propose further intervention for now
